product_name: Magnum Opus Scholarly Retrieval And Warrant System
final_object_type: local scholarly retrieval and evidence-warrant software
runtime_target: local corpus service with index, retrieval trace, scoring, negative-space, abstention, and citation/export layer
operational_end_state: A researcher can ask a question against a local scholarly corpus, inspect retrieved passages, see what the corpus does not support, receive an evidence score, and export only claims that remain inside the source warrant.
full_build_definition: A runnable local or web application with a defined user flow, input fixture, persisted or exportable output, source-bound mechanism, acceptance tests, and a reviewer-visible claim ceiling.
minimum_operational_release: A runnable slice of Magnum Opus Scholarly Retrieval And Warrant System: one complete workflow from input to output, with fixture, state record, export, acceptance test, and source-bound claim boundary.
required_modules_to_build: corpus registry | index and retrieval service | passage trace viewer | evidence scoring | negative-space detector | abstention policy | citation/export boundary
source_status: 6 source-application rows; 0 active passage contracts; 0 unlocated source candidates.
source_actions_before_claim: Carry 6 source-application role(s) into the first build release and convert the most load-bearing source into a close-read contract before any quotation, page-specific citation, or strong literature-positioning claim.
test_suite: claim ceiling test: public claims cannot exceed built evidence | source test: metadata-routed sources require close reading before citation or quotation | privacy test: no local paths or private directories in public deploy | retrieval replay test: clean and contaminated fixtures produce inspectable traces | state-transition test: visible input changes recorded state and output
acceptance_tests: Open or run the application slice; feed the fixture; verify output artifact, state persistence or export, error boundary, and source-governed acceptance condition.
promotion_stage: prototype-to-full-build; source and capture checks still govern release language
current_status: specified proof plan; needs live or replayable demonstrator
launch_or_review_path: Create or open the local runtime slice for `native-magnum-opus`, then execute the fixture-to-output workflow and capture the result.
first_full_build: Build a public-safe retrieval trace with corpus boundary, retrieved passages, evidence score, negative-space finding, abstention, and claim ceiling.
next_implementation_steps: 1. create or confirm runtime data schema; 2. implement one end-to-end fixture; 3. capture proof artifact; 4. run source and claim-ceiling gates; 5. publish reviewer-safe demo
public_claim_now: May claim a prototype-to-full-build path exists; do not claim completed operational system.
claim_boundary: This is a full-build plan, not a claim that the full operational system has already been completed.
